name: Sierra de Hornachuelos Loop from Peñaflor
name_original: Desde Peñaflor hasta el Embalse del Retortillo – circular por La Puebla de los Infantes
description: This moderate 31.6-mile road cycling route from Peñaflor to Embalse del Retortillo offers scenic views and some unpaved sections.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Peñaflor offer a diverse landscape in the Santiago Metropolitan region of Chile, catering to various fitness levels. The area features varied terrain, including well-paved roads and segments with significant elevation gains, alongside natural features like the Mapocho River and the Humedal Urbano El Trapiche. Cyclists can expect a mix of challenging climbs and more gentle routes through rural landscapes. The region's topography provides a dynamic environment for road cycling. Best road cycling routes around Peñaflor The most popular road cycling route is Sierra de Hornachuelos Loop from Peñaflor, a 31.6 miles (50.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 17 minutes to complete, featuring notable elevation gain through rolling hills. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Hornachuelos from Palma del Rio — circular by Posadas, a moderate 34.7 miles (55.8 km) path with varied terrain and scenic views of the surrounding countryside. Local road cyclists also love the From Palma del Rio to La Puebla de los Infantes – circular via Peñaflor, a 31.9 miles (51.3 km) trail leading through rural landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 20 minutes. Road cycling around Peñaflor is defined by diverse landscapes, varied terrain, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to difficult routes with significant climbs. The routes in Peñaflor are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from 1 review. More than 400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Peñaflor's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Peñaflor?
A: Peñaflor offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 30 options available. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy rides to challenging climbs.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Peñaflor?
A: The region is particularly popular for road cycling during &lt;strong&gt;November and December&lt;/strong&gt;, offering favorable conditions to explore the diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Peñaflor?
A: Yes, Peñaflor has several routes suitable for beginners. There are 3 easy routes available, offering a gentler introduction to road cycling in the area. These routes often feature mostly well-paved surfaces, ideal for those new to the sport.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Peñaflor?
A: The terrain in Peñaflor is diverse. You'll find routes with &lt;strong&gt;mostly well-paved surfaces&lt;/strong&gt;, perfect for smooth rides. However, some routes, especially those with significant elevation gains like parts of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31869951" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñaflor – Retortillo Reservoir loop from Palma del Río&lt;/a&gt; or the Cuesta de Mallarauco, may include &lt;strong&gt;unpaved or difficult-to-ride segments&lt;/strong&gt;, adding a challenge for experienced riders.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders in Peñaflor?
A: Absolutely. Peñaflor is known for its challenging routes with significant elevation gains. For instance, the Cuesta de Mallarauco is a tough climb, and routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31869951" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñaflor – Retortillo Reservoir loop from Palma del Río&lt;/a&gt; offer substantial ascents, requiring very good fitness. There are 8 difficult routes available for those seeking a demanding ride.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes around Peñaflor?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Peñaflor are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995742932/sierra-de-hornachuelos-loop-from-penaflor" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Hornachuelos Loop from Peñaflor&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010641055/hornachuelos-from-palma-del-rio-circular-by-posadas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hornachuelos from Palma del Rio — circular by Posadas&lt;/a&gt;, which offer varied terrain and scenic views.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along road cycling routes in Peñaflor?
A: Peñaflor's routes often pass by significant natural features. You can explore areas near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3415384" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñaflor Reservoir - Guadalquivir River&lt;/a&gt;, which is a municipal natural reserve, or the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5617701" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bembézar Dam&lt;/a&gt;. The Humedal Urbano El Trapiche is another unique ecosystem worth noting, providing a habitat for diverse bird species.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints on the road cycling routes in Peñaflor?
A: Yes, the region offers several scenic spots. The Cuesta Mallarauco, for example, leads to a viewpoint offering expansive vistas. Additionally, you might encounter views of reservoirs like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/564940" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buendía Reservoir from Mirador de Las Palomas&lt;/a&gt;, providing picturesque breaks during your ride.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Peñaflor?
A: The komoot community highly rates the diverse landscapes and varied terrain of Peñaflor. Cyclists often praise the mix of challenging climbs and more gentle routes through rural areas, as well as the natural beauty of the Mapocho River and Humedal Urbano El Trapiche.

Q: Are there any specific landmarks or historical sites near the road cycling routes?
A: Beyond natural beauty, you can find interesting landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3938708" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de los Presos del Bajo Guadalquivir&lt;/a&gt; or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7163453" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hotel Monasterio de San Francisco&lt;/a&gt;. These offer glimpses into the region's heritage as you cycle through.

Q: Can I find long-distance road cycling trails near Peñaflor?
A: Yes, Peñaflor serves as a starting point or part of several longer routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31869951" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñaflor – Retortillo Reservoir loop from Palma del Río&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging long-distance option, covering over 113 km with significant elevation gain, ideal for endurance riders.

Q: Are there options for moderate difficulty road cycling routes?
A: The majority of road cycling routes in Peñaflor are of moderate difficulty, with 22 such routes available. These routes, like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995740223/from-palma-del-rio-to-la-puebla-de-los-infantes-circular-via-penaflor" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Palma del Rio to La Puebla de los Infantes – circular via Peñaflor&lt;/a&gt;, offer a good balance of distance and elevation, suitable for cyclists with a reasonable fitness level.
tips: Built in 1970, its large hydrographic basin is dominated by the typical Mediterranean forest of holm oaks and cork oaks. Here you can see cormorants, herons and various species of ducks.

It is located in the highest part of the town of La Puebla de los Infantes. The Gothic-Mudejar style castle was built between 1330 and 1344. It is curious that the houses built later, in the 18th and 19th centuries, are "attached" to the castle - they took advantage of its walls to build them.

Sevillian town in the foothills of Sierra Morena. The church of San Pedro Apóstol stands out, declared an Asset of Cultural Interest for being located between baroque and neoclassicism, and the Casa Palacio - an emblematic baroque building for its high historical value in civil architecture.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this racebike route?
faqs.answer: This moderate 50.9 km racebike route typically takes around 2 hours and 17 minutes to complete. This duration can vary based on your cycling pace, fitness level, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is this racebike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The route is categorized as 'moderate' with an elevation gain of over 540 meters, requiring good fitness. While not extremely challenging, beginners might find some sections demanding, especially if they are not accustomed to longer distances or elevation changes. Some unpaved segments could also pose a challenge for traditional road bikes.

faqs.question: Where can I start this racebike route and find parking?
faqs.answer: This is a circular route, so you can start in either Peñaflor or La Puebla de los Infantes. Both towns offer parking facilities. For example, you can find parking in the town center of Peñaflor or La Puebla de los Infantes.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers several scenic highlights. You'll cycle past the beautiful Retortillo Reservoir (also known as Pantano del Águila), and pass through the charming towns of Peñaflor and La Puebla de los Infantes, where you can see the Castle of La Puebla de los Infantes.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this racebike route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, taking you through the natural beauty of the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park. You'll experience dehesas (wooded pastures), olive groves, and cultivated fields. A crucial detail for road cyclists is that some segments of this route may be unpaved and difficult to ride, which might require a more robust road bike or hybrid, or a willingness to tackle mixed terrain.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route in Andalusia is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. Temperatures are milder, and the landscape is vibrant. Summers can be very hot, making cycling less comfortable, while winters can be cooler and wetter.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route?
faqs.answer: This route passes through the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park, which is part of the larger Parque Natural Sierra de Hornachuelos. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public roads within the park. However, always respect local regulations and private property.

faqs.question: Is this racebike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route primarily follows public roads, bringing dogs on a racebike can be challenging. If you plan to cycle with a dog, ensure they are safely secured in a bike trailer or basket. Always keep dogs on a leash when off the bike, especially in natural park areas, to protect wildlife and ensure their safety.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. You will find yourself on sections of the Sendero de Sierra Morena, CHG - Embalse del Retortillo, and Pista Cordel de Hornachuelos y Peñaflor at various points along the way.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or resupply along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, both Peñaflor and La Puebla de los Infantes offer cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can resupply with food and water. It's always a good idea to carry enough provisions for the ride, especially between towns, as services might be limited.

faqs.question: What gear do you recommend for this racebike route?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and potential for unpaved sections, we recommend a well-maintained racebike, or even a gravel bike or hybrid for added comfort on mixed terrain. Essential gear includes a helmet, water bottles, repair kit, appropriate clothing for the weather, and navigation tools. Don't forget sunscreen and sunglasses, especially during sunnier months.

waypoints.name: Castle of La Puebla de los Infantes
